| performance | failed | — | ⊗ infrastructure failure |

## Blocking Findings

### [infrastructure] All four convoy reviewers exited with code 1 without writing reports

All four sub-role reviewers (security, correctness, performance, requirements) failed to produce review output. Each terminated with exit code 1 and no report file was written. This is a blocking infrastructure failure per the review protocol: reviewer failures block approval regardless of the nature of the failure.

**Evidence:**
- `REVIEWER_FAILED security reviewer exited (code 1) without writing report`
- `REVIEWER_FAILED correctness reviewer exited (code 1) without writing report`
- `REVIEWER_FAILED performance reviewer exited (code 1) without writing report`
- `REVIEWER_FAILED requirements reviewer exited (code 1) without writing report`

No reviewer output files exist at the expected paths:
- `.pan/review/agent-pan-1052-review-1a8e38d5/security.md`
- `.pan/review/agent-pan-1052-review-1a8e38d5/correctness.md`
- `.pan/review/agent-pan-1052-review-1a8e38d5/performance.md`
- `.pan/review/agent-pan-1052-review-1a8e38d5/requirements.md`

**Root cause: unknown.** Reviewer failure reasons were not captured in the failure signals. Possible causes include runtime errors in the reviewer prompts, resource exhaustion, or execution policy blocks. Investigation of the reviewer subprocess logs is required before re-review can proceed.

## Non-blocking Findings

None — all sub-roles are blocked by infrastructure failure.

## Scope Note

All four reviewers failed. This is not a proportionality or scope issue — the review cannot proceed at all without reviewer output.

## Clean Sub-roles

None. All four sub-roles produced infrastructure failures.

Source: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1052/.pan/review/agent-pan-1052-review-1a8e38d5/synthesis.md

## Required action

Fix every blocking review finding, commit the fixes, then re-request review with:

`pan review request PAN-1052 -m "Fixed review issues"`
